plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and meet the single-name cap.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs as per conservative mode.
Add to QQQI to increase income exposure and QQQ for broad index.
Trim CRWD to 10% to meet single-name cap.
Buy XLV at 5% to diversify into healthcare, a top-quality name.
This plan reduces overall leverage and concentration significantly, favoring capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation and low drawdown. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and diversifying, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and meet the single-name cap.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero.
Trim QQQI to 15% to rebalance the portfolio.
Buy AMAT at 10% and add to CRWD to 15% to increase exposure to quality tech with strong setups.
Buy RTX at 5% to diversify with a strong risk/reward setup.
This plan balances broad index, quality tech, and reduced leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month, aiming for risk-first growth by reducing leverage and diversifying. It maintains a moderate risk profile with a focus on quality tech and broad market exposure.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and meet the single-name cap.
Trim QQQI to 10% to free up capital for growth-oriented assets.
Add to SMH to 20% and CRWD to 20% to press into strong tech and AI/Software.
Add to SOXL to 7% and buy AMAT at 10% to increase leveraged semiconductor exposure, with strict stops.
Buy RTX at 8% to add a strong risk/reward setup.
This plan aggressively leans into high-scoring setups and uses leveraged ETFs for stretched growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month, stretching for growth by increasing leveraged ETF exposure and concentrating in high-scoring setups. This mode significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential; leveraged ETFs decay daily, and strict stop discipline is critical to manage rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and meet the single-name cap.
Sell SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to exit all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Add to QQQI to 40% to maximize income exposure.
Trim QQQ to 10% to further prioritize income over growth.
This plan rotates heavily into income-generating ETFs and broad index for steady distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable monthly distributions. While aiming for steady income, distributions are variable, and the NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
